p.seg: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future, Geoffrey son of Waldef of Lilliesleaf sends greeting.

Know me to have given and granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks serving God in that place, in free, pure, and perpetual alms, four oxgangs of arable land in the territory of Whitton, just as they lie in one holding above ‘Raven’s Fen’, near the land of the same monks which they have from the donation of Patrick of Ryedale my lord; this whole land and whatever is comprised within the said boundaries of the land I have given to the said monks of Melrose free, quit, and loosed from every earthly service and custom and secular exaction, that they may have, hold, and possess them for ever as freely, fully and honourably as any alms can be freely, quitely, fully and honourably held and possessed. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant to the said monks the same land against all men, and discharge for ever all forinsec service towards our lords, and all other things which can be exacted for any land.

With these witnesses: Robert, archdeacon of Glasgow; Patrick of Ryedale my lord; Walter and Nicholas, his sons; Richard le Naym; Ralph of Ryedale; Richard, parson of Ancrum; Robert of Barnoldby; Adam of Whitton; William of Whitton; Adam of Chatto; and many others.
